title: stachys palustris subsp. pilosa
intro: Stachys palustris subsp. pilosa, also known as hairy marsh woundwort, is a subspecies of perennial herb native to Europe. It is a member of the mint family and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. Stachys palustris subsp. pilosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in topical applications to aid in the treatment of various ailments.
